perturbation.original_text: Aduro Biotech, Inc., with a place of business at 740 Heinz Avenue, Berkeley, CA 94710 ("Aduro") and IREYA B.V having an address at Staalwijkstraat 16, 2313 XR Leiden, the Netherlands, represented by Andrea van Elsas, ("Consultant") agree to all terms and conditions of this Consulting Agreement ("Agreement") dated June 1, 2020, effective as of July 1, 2020 ("Effective Date").
perturbation.changed_text: Aduro Biotech, Inc., with a place of business at 740 Heinz Avenue, Berkeley, CA 94710 ("Aduro") and IREYA B.V having an address at Staalwijkstraat 16, 2313 XR Leiden, the Netherlands, represented by Andrea van Elsas, ("Independent Professional") agree to all terms and conditions of this Consulting Agreement ("Agreement") dated June 1, 2020, effective as of July 1, 2020 ("Effective Date").
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Consultant' to 'Independent Professional' introduces ambiguity. While seemingly synonymous, the contract continues to use 'Consultant' in subsequent sections, creating uncertainty as to whether all obligations apply equally to the 'Independent Professional'. This can cause issues in sections like the Confidentiality Clause and Intellectual Property rights.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential Information. (a) "Confidential Information" means any information, materials or methods in whatever form or embodiment that has not been made available by Aduro to the general public and any information, materials or methods in the possession or control of Consultant on the Effective Date or developed in the performance of the Services, except that Confidential Information shall not include any information, material or method that (i) at the time of disclosure is in, or after disclosure becomes part of the public domain, through no improper act on the part of Consultant or any of its employees; (ii) was in Consultant's possession at the time of disclosure, as shown by written evidence, and was not acquired, directly or indirectly, from work with Aduro; or (iii) Consultant receives from a third party, provided that such Confidential Information was not obtained by such third party, directly or indirectly, from Aduro. Specific information disclosed as part of the Confidential Information shall not be deemed to be in the public domain or in the prior possession of Consultant merely because it is encompassed or contemplated by more general information in the public domain or in the prior possession of the Consultant. Failure to mark any of the Confidential Information as confidential or proprietary shall not affect its status as Confidential Information under the terms of this Agreement. (b) Consultant shall keep all Confidential Information confidential, and Consultant shall not disclose, disseminate, publish, reproduce or use Confidential Information except to perform the Services. If Consultant is required by judicial or administrative process to disclose Confidential Information, Consultant shall promptly notify Aduro to allow Aduro a reasonable time to oppose such process and Consultant shall reasonably cooperate in Aduro's efforts. (c) On Aduro's request, or upon the termination or expiration of this Agreement, Consultant shall immediately: (i) stop using Confidential Information; (ii) return all materials provided by Aduro to Consultant that contain Confidential Information, except for one copy that may be retained by Consultant's legal counsel to confirm compliance with the obligations under this Agreement; (iii) destroy all copies of Confidential Information in any form including Confidential Information contained in computer memory or data storage apparatus or materials prepared by or for Consultant; and (iv) provide a written warranty to Aduro that Consultant has taken all the actions described in the foregoing Subparagraphs 4(c)(i-iii). (d) Any breach of this Paragraph 4 by an employee or agent of Consultant shall be deemed to be a breach by Consultant. (e) Defend Trade Secrets Act Notice: Nothing herein shall prevent Consultant from reporting possible violations of federal or state law or regulation to any governmental agency or entity, or making other disclosures that are protected under the whistleblower provisions of federal or state law or regulation. Consultant does not need the prior authorization of Aduro to make any such reports or disclosures and is not required to notify Aduro that it has made such reports or disclosures. In addition, as set forth in 18 U.S.C. §1833(b), Consultant shall not be held criminally or civilly liable under any federal or state trade secret law for the disclosure of a trade secret that is made in confidence to a federal, state or local government official, either directly or indirectly, or to an attorney, and that is made solely for the purpose of reporting or investigating a suspected violation of law, or that is made in a complaint or other document filed in a lawsuit or other proceeding if such filing is made under seal.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Consultant' to 'Independent Professional' introduces misaligned terminology. The contradicted text uses 'Consultant' extensively when outlining obligations related to Confidential Information. Because the changed text redefines 'Consultant' as 'Independent Professional' but the contradicted section references 'Consultant', there is an in-text contradiction causing uncertainty about whether all obligations apply equally to the 'Independent Professional', particularly in the Confidentiality clause which states obligations of the 'Consultant'.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Invoices shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.changed_text: The Independent Contractor's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Payment requests shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Invoices' to 'Payment requests', implies, that if the 'Consultant' and 'Independent Contractor' are two different parties and only one of them is required to provide invoice, but not the other, which is suppose to only provide payment requests, it creates ambiguity about the invoicing procedure. And what the 'consultant' is suppose to do in this case. Especially because the definitions were changed in the text earlier.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Compensation and Expenses. Aduro shall pay Consultant for the Services at the rate of €500 per hour. On a monthly basis, Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice for the hours worked along with itemized documentation and receipts and other information for pre-approved travel and/or out-of- pocket expenses as Aduro reasonably requests at the time reimbursement is requested. Consultant will not incur any travel and/or other out-of-pocket expenses of more than €5,000 individually or €20,000 in the aggregate without the prior written consent of Aduro. Aduro shall pay Consultant any amounts due that are not reasonably disputed by Aduro, by check or direct bank deposit, within thirty days after receiving the invoice. Consultant's sole compensation for the Services shall be the amounts set forth above in this Section 2. Payment requests shall be sent to the attention of: ap@aduro.com Attn: Accounts Payable
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Invoices' to 'Payment requests' introduces ambiguity regarding the required documentation for compensation. The Contradicted Text explicitly states 'Consultant shall submit to Aduro an invoice...', but the Changed Text replaces 'Invoices' with 'Payment requests' for 'Independent Contractor', potentially implying that the 'Consultant' (if different from the 'Independent Contractor') might not need to submit invoices, creating a contradiction regarding the invoicing procedure mentioned in the Contradicted Text. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where interchangeable terms without definition lead to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Consultant shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes.
perturbation.changed_text: The Service Provider shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the term 'Consultant' to 'Service Provider' creates ambiguity about the responsible party. While Section 5 previously mentioned Consultant, now it introduces another term without clarification if the two terms are referring to the same person. This introduces uncertainty to the contract.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Independent Contractor. Consultant's relationship to Aduro shall be that of an independent contractor. The Service Provider shall be responsible for the timely payment of his or her own self-employment and income taxes. Neither party shall have any authority to bind the other.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Consultant' to 'Service Provider' introduces ambiguity as to which entity is responsible for tax payments. The contradicted text also includes the statement 'Consultant's relationship to Aduro shall be that of an independent contractor,' but the changed text introduces a second term 'Service Provider', which is not defined, and creates uncertainty about whether they are the same entity. The 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition states that in-text contradictions occur when the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations, which is exactly what happens here, as it becomes unclear if both terms refer to the same entity or two different ones.
